comment: Advance widths MUST be one of the values in allowed_widths. iA Writer Quattro uses four canonical width classes (300/450/600/900) plus a few outlier widths on precomposed accent glyphs. Run 'scripts/inspect_font.py' on the source fonts to verify.

modifications._comment: Map glyph names to new advance widths. All values must be in allowed_widths above. Run scripts/inspect.py to see current widths and glyph names.

fraction_rebuild._comment: Replace precomposed fraction glyphs with composites of <num>.numr + fraction + <den>.dnom at advance_width. reshape_fraction_slash rewrites the `fraction` glyph itself as a single continuous slash (preserving its 8-point/2-contour gvar structure so the weight axis still works). layout.auto=true computes per-target x-offsets from actual component ink bounds so each composite is centered; layout.gap adds horizontal space between numerator ink end and denominator ink start (larger = wider fractions).
